doom.driver: North Korea updates constitution to require automatic nuclear strike if Kim Jong Un is assassinated: report.
doom.blurb: DEAD DICTATOR TRIGGERS END OF WORLD, AUTOMATICALLY
doom.summary: North Korea has apparently decided that human judgment is too slow for nuclear war and hardwired 'Kim dies = everyone dies' directly into their constitution. Meanwhile, the Iran war grinds on with cargo ships catching fire off Qatar and the Strait of Hormuz swallowing roughly a billion barrels of oil, so we're juggling multiple live grenades today. The hantavirus cruise ship chaos is genuinely unpleasant but the CDC assures us it won't go pandemic — which is exactly what you'd say right before it goes pandemic.

factors.name: Nuclear
factors.detail: North Korea constitutionally mandating an automatic nuclear strike on Kim's death removes human deliberation from the most consequential decision imaginable.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: Iran war continues to roil energy markets, with Aramco profit surging 26% while the Hormuz blockade swallows a billion barrels and a cargo ship burns off Qatar.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: Trump-Xi summit looms with crude oil as a covert bargaining chip, and Starmer survives a crushing Labour defeat while facing an internal leadership threat by Monday.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: AI news is mostly internal Meta misery and LLM document-corruption research — concerning for the long run, quiet for today.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Hantavirus cruise ship evacuation dominates headlines; CDC says low pandemic risk, but person-to-person transmission and global travel routes keep it on the board.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing — weather-dominated interior scene viewed through the rain-lashed tall windows of the Mansudae Assembly Hall, Pyongyang, the camera positioned outside in the cold looking inward through streaked glass thick with condensation and running rivulets, the massive red-and-gold DPRK state emblem dominating the far wall of the chamber — hydroelectric dam, mountain peak, red star wreathed in rice stalks — rendered in deep smoldering orange against dark wood paneling, the fluorescent overhead lights cutting cold white columns through the heavy shadow of the interior, a heavy red-covered document lying open on a legislative podium in the foreground with a gold state seal embossed on its cover, a rubber stamp suspended mid-descent above it catching the orange light, the tiered rows of institutional seating stretching back in shadow behind, outside the glass a grey Pyongyang ceremonial boulevard dissolves into driving winter rain, the pavement mirror-black with standing water reflecting smears of orange and red light from the hall windows, dense low cloud pressing down over the pale brutalist towers in the distance, deep navy sky bleeding into charcoal, the entire scene filtered through rain and fog and threat, warmth present only as warning.

dawn.driver: Scientists just sent unhackable quantum keys across 120 kilometers. Scientists have taken a major step toward ultra-secure quantum communication by demonstrating a remarkably stable quantum encryption system that worked across more than 120 kilometers of optical fiber
dawn.blurb: HUMANITY LOCKS THE DOOR ON SURVEILLANCE FOREVER
dawn.summary: In a world where digital trust is eroding on every front — AI manipulation, VPN crackdowns, encryption rollbacks — scientists have quietly achieved something remarkable: genuinely unhackable communication across 120 kilometers of fiber, the longest stable quantum key distribution yet demonstrated. This isn't theoretical. The laws of physics themselves make interception detectable, meaning this technology, once scaled, could make mass surveillance cryptographically impossible. Against a backdrop of geopolitical instability and rising authoritarian digital control, the fact that researchers are handing the world this gift — privacy as a law of nature, not a policy — is a genuine reason for optimism.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: Quantum key distribution across 120km of fiber marks a major practical milestone toward unbreakable real-world communications infrastructure.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: Hungary's new PM Péter Magyar pledges service over rule after sweeping out 16 years of authoritarian governance — democratic renewal still happens.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: Cape Verde's tiny nation is transforming lives through World Cup qualification, showing sport as a genuine engine of communal hope.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: The 2022 Hunga Tonga eruption cloud unexpectedly destroyed atmospheric methane, stunning scientists and hinting at poorly understood natural climate feedback mechanisms.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Scientists reversed liver aging in mice using young gut bacteria, opening a credible new front in the effort to extend healthy human lifespan.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Extreme close-up filling the entire frame with a single ornate skeleton key, its bow and blade rendered in deep warm amber and molten gold pixel-light, the metal surface alive with a faint inner luminescence as though photons are trapped inside the brass itself. The key floats against pure darkness, but its polished shaft reflects two ghostly teal-green glowing lines — thin as fiber threads — that curve along its surface and vanish into the black edges of the frame, implying vast invisible distance beyond the composition. The key's teeth are impossibly precise, geometric, catching side-light from a deep golden source at hard left, casting one long dramatic shadow rightward across the dark void. A single pixel-bright amber spark sits at the very tip of the blade, small as a quantum ember, radiating a soft halo of warm gold into the surrounding black. The key's surface shows faint interference patterns in deep teal and violet — wavelike, mathematical, unhackable. Radiant side-lighting, long shadows, deep golden hour amber and warm teal palette, bioluminescent glow quality, hushed and monumental.
